title: Best Road Trip Snacks for Kids in NZ — No-Mess, No-Meltdown Options
meta_desc: Discover the best no-mess NZ road trip snacks for kids to avoid meltdowns on long drives
intro: A 4-hour drive from Auckland to Rotorua can be a nightmare if your kids are hungry and restless. Packing the right snacks can make all the difference. Here are some practical ideas for no-mess, no-meltdown road trip snacks in NZ.

sections.heading: Practical Snack Ideas
sections.body: When choosing snacks for a long road trip in NZ, consider non-perishable items that are easy to eat on-the-go. Fresh fruits like apples ($1.50 each at Countdown supermarkets) and oranges ($2.00 per kilogram at Pak'nSave) are great options. You can also pack sandwiches, crackers, and cheese for a satisfying snack.

sections.heading: Supermarket Options
list: Whittaker's chocolate bars ($2.50 each at New World supermarkets)
Kettle Chips ($3.00 per packet at Four Square stores)
Muesli bars ($1.00 each at The Warehouse stores)

sections.heading: Cooler Tips
sections.body: If you plan to pack perishable items like yogurt, meats, or snacks that require refrigeration, invest in a good cooler. You can find coolers at outdoor stores like Kathmandu or Macpac, with prices starting from $50.00. Fill your cooler with ice packs ($5.00 each at Bunnings Warehouse) and keep your snacks fresh for hours.

sections.heading: Avoiding Meltdowns
sections.body: A long drive from Wellington to Queenstown (around 10 hours) can be challenging for kids. To avoid meltdowns, pack a variety of snacks and offer them at regular intervals. You can also consider downloading kid-friendly apps or games on your tablet to keep them entertained during the journey.
